121743.explanation: A referendum turns a complicated question into a yes or no choice. As a municipal council, we are specifically elected to make these complex considerations. However, we do like to involve residents in the decision-making process.

121749.explanation: Labor migrants can also be housed by others and in different locations. It is important that these are safe homes and that no nuisance occurs. Employers bear greater responsibility for this.

121752.explanation: A fireworks show is not a task of the municipality and therefore we do not spend taxpayer money on it.

121753.explanation: In Oudewater, you can bring bulky waste to the landfill for free. As far as we are concerned, you pay the extra costs for home collection yourself. These costs should be low so that everyone can afford it.

121754.explanation: There are many fun parties in Oudewater. The current noise standards limit the nuisance from them. Enforcing the current standards is important. This is still going wrong too often.

121756.explanation: Only when expanding a neighborhood does the municipality choose a location where building on agricultural land is possible. Preferably, the municipality buys that land.

121759.explanation: Our school buildings must always be in good condition. If investments are needed for this, we will make them.

121760.explanation: As a municipality, we encourage the development of children. We only contribute financially if parents cannot afford it themselves.

121763.explanation: As CDA, we attach great value to safety in our municipality. Wrongly parked cars still prevent the fire brigade from passing. There are also various places where residents feel unsafe.

121764.explanation: Safety comes first. Fortunately, crime is low in Oudewater. In places with nuisance, we place cameras to prevent street crime, trace offenders, and increase safety.

121765.explanation: These types of shelter facilities are arranged regionally. Oudewater is too small for this, making it impossible for the housing and residents to remain anonymous.

121766.explanation: The municipality must help starters with buying a home in every possible way.

121769.explanation: If their health permits, we help residents get back into the labor market. In return for benefits, we ask them to do volunteer work.

121771.explanation: This was already done recently at the request of CDA. We are monitoring developments. If poverty continues to increase, we will raise the threshold again.

121773.explanation: We create more charging points on demand from users. So we only do this when the points will actually be used.

121774.explanation: Driving at 30 km/h significantly increases safety for pedestrians and cyclists. We find this adjustment important, for example in Hekendorp and Papekop.

121775.explanation: With respect for each other's differences, everyone can help one another. As CDA, we find it important that you can choose for yourself who helps you.

121780.explanation: The Sunday shopping in Oudewater currently works well. It makes Oudewater attractive for tourism while causing little nuisance.

121781.explanation: Refugees are a vulnerable group in our society who cannot find housing without assistance. To contribute to national reception efforts, we need priority allocation. We also give priority allocation to our own residents.

121782.explanation: Solar energy on land is one of the methods to generate energy. The alternative of wind turbines is less attractive. We first cover large roofs and then consider what is needed on land.

121783.explanation: Many children in Oudewater take swimming lessons and have swimming certificates. Therefore, it is not necessary to encourage this through school swimming.

121786.explanation: Building tall apartment blocks does not fit in Oudewater. We will not build higher than what already exists.

121787.explanation: Room rental is possible in certain situations. This helps solve part of the housing demand. However, we limit additional parking pressure.

121789.explanation: The shopping streets must become car-free so that safety and shopping pleasure increase. It also has a positive effect on tourism. There will be a loading and unloading period for shops.

121790.explanation: Without cooperation, Oudewater cannot exist as a small municipality and quality will decline.

121791.explanation: The imposition of wind turbines in Papekop is against the wishes of the municipal council. A solar field should be placed there instead. Residents agree with this.

121793.explanation: The housing shortage in Oudewater is high. Transforming yards or small buildings in the rural area helps build more homes.

121794.explanation: The current plans for a new swimming pool cost 2.9 million euros in a one-time contribution and an average of 300,000 euros per year in tax money from residents. On top of that, the current plan carries significant financial risks.

121743.explanation: Participation is important, but we also want to address problems quickly. With our many work visits and active supporters, we know well what is happening in Oudewater. We trust that we can carry out good representation.

121749.explanation: Labor migrants and seasonal workers are necessary for some of our entrepreneurs. Good housing for them is a prerequisite. Entrepreneurs, homeowners, and employment agencies can offer good solutions themselves. No mandatory housing on the company’s own premises.

121752.explanation: A central fireworks show is not a task for the municipality. We are also not necessarily in favor of abolishing yet another Dutch tradition. Illegal fireworks do jeopardize safety and are not tolerable, but a complete ban is patronizing.

121753.explanation: If you have bulky waste, you must dispose of it yourself and not let the other residents bear the costs. We do want longer opening hours for the recycling center, for example at Oudewater’s local waste processor.

121754.explanation: Standards for festivals and parties are becoming increasingly strict. If organizers meet national standards, that is good and the municipality should not make it more difficult.

121756.explanation: If farmers choose to do so themselves, we see this as a good contribution to solving the housing shortage.

121759.explanation: Quality education and good, safe school buildings are important. That is why we continue to invest in school facilities.

121760.explanation: If parents cannot afford music lessons for their children, they should be eligible for subsidies.

121763.explanation: More police presence on the streets for safety is good. On VVD Oudewater’s initiative, more has been invested in this. Enforcement officers should focus on increasing safety.

121764.explanation: The safety of our residents is important and cameras contribute to this by their preventive effect.

121765.explanation: Residents in unsafe situations should have somewhere to go when necessary.

121766.explanation: We wish every Oudewater resident to have their own home. This measure can help achieve that.

121769.explanation: Oudewater offers a safety net, not a hammock. People with benefits can make a great social contribution, and participation helps.

121771.explanation: Work must pay off and a higher income threshold hinders that.

121773.explanation: The number of charging points should be balanced with the number of electric cars in Oudewater. The municipality is responsible for the spatial planning and therefore for designating locations.

121774.explanation: On main roads such as Johan J. Vierbergenweg and Zwier Regelinkstraat, 30 km/h is too slow and simply annoying. Within residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h is important for the safety of children and other residents.

121775.explanation: The municipality must provide appropriate and effective care.

121780.explanation: VVD Oudewater wants extended shopping hours. Entrepreneurs and residents should be able to decide for themselves on Sundays.

121781.explanation: VVD Oudewater supports equal opportunities. Residents who have been registered for years are wrongly passed over for priority allocation to status holders.

121782.explanation: If a landowner sees opportunities for solar panels on agricultural land, the municipality should not prohibit this outright.

121783.explanation: School swimming is important for the safety and sportsmanship of our youth. It also offers freedom to parents. The new 12-month swimming pool provides the facilities for this.

121786.explanation: High residential towers do not suit the rural character of Oudewater. A residential flat like Schuylenburcht we find appropriate.

121787.explanation: Renting out rooms contributes to solving the housing shortage. The youth of Oudewater can thus more quickly build their own independent future.

121789.explanation: The center is currently low-traffic for cars. This means that entrepreneurs can be supplied and residents can do their shopping. In summer the inner city closes in the afternoons for terrace visitors. We want to keep it that way.

121790.explanation: Oudewater’s independence comes first. For this, it is necessary that we cooperate with other municipalities to purchase administrative expertise. This does not necessarily have to be with the municipality of Woerden.

121791.explanation: We were, are, and remain opposed to wind turbines in Papekop. The province of Utrecht made a big mistake by mapping these wind turbines in Papekop against the will of our residents.

121793.explanation: Building on the city edge is cheaper and easier than building in the inner city. VVD Oudewater has been advocating this for years, and the council must keep pressuring the province to make this possible.

121794.explanation: A new swimming pool is an important sports facility and contributes to the mental and physical health of our residents. The citizen initiative for the new swimming pool is unique and deserves the municipality's support.

121743.explanation: It is very important that residents can always participate in discussions, especially on important topics. This does not have to be through a referendum; participation is more than just voting. Major issues involve more than just for or against. Referenda miss out on arguments and possibilities.

121749.explanation: We have insufficient land and horticulture. However, we do believe that labor migrants should be accommodated properly so that they cannot be immediately expelled when they lose their jobs. Good housing is a shared responsibility of employers and the municipality.

121752.explanation: We prefer a laser show; it is more sustainable. It prevents a lot of nuisance in the city center and is a nice way to meet each other and wish a happy new year. Let's organize a great celebration.

121753.explanation: Collection should be free. The cost of waste should be equal to the cost of the recycling center. Even better, we prefer that large items are reused through thrift stores or church flea markets.

121754.explanation: In our view, there are standards in accordance with regulations to prevent hearing damage. Before tightening them, it seems better to us to enforce the current standards more strictly.

121756.explanation: If it is a good plan that fits the landscape and provides housing needed for target groups such as starters, it should be possible in good consultation.

121759.explanation: We have relatively new schools. The current financial budgets suit this. However, we need to consider the climate in schools. In the future, more cooling will be necessary, and the quality of the air will become increasingly important for our children and teachers.

121760.explanation: It is better to further stimulate and support music school/education. Where necessary, people with a minimal income can pay through the Groene Hart Pass. We would like to broaden this to include art or art initiatives, for example through cultural education.

121763.explanation: We have enough community enforcement officers, but they are still insufficiently visible.

121764.explanation: We are a small community with a lot of social control. The safety figures also do not indicate that this is necessary. However, the community enforcement officers and police should be more visible. We want to maintain or strengthen social cohesion, so that camera surveillance will not be necessary.

121765.explanation: It is important that they exist, but such things require anonymity, which is difficult to realize in Oudewater. It seems better to us that people who need this know where to go.

121766.explanation: We must do everything to help people find an affordable first home.

121769.explanation: If this is possible, it is desirable for society, but especially for the people themselves, so they can continue to participate in society and give meaningful purpose to their existence.

121771.explanation: There is a lot of hidden poverty. It is important that children get a good start.

121773.explanation: There is already a shortage of regular parking spaces. We understand the energy transition. It is better to encourage charging at home (with panels). Then these spaces remain public and accessible to everyone.

121774.explanation: For safety, this is better, especially in the city center.

121780.explanation: In Oudewater, we leave it up to the entrepreneurs/shopkeepers whether they want to be open on Sundays. Some shops are open and are sufficiently used. We have no arguments to reduce this.

121781.explanation: We understand the legal obligation and would like to accommodate these people. It is important that we are then also allowed to build homes outside the red lines, so there are enough homes for everyone.

121782.explanation: Carefully and in consultation with residents, searching for the right location. Prefer panels over wind turbines.

121783.explanation: Almost all our children go to swimming lessons, and if this is not financially possible, it can be paid for with the Groene Hart Pass. More important is that swimming lessons can be followed nearby.

121786.explanation: We think of gallery apartments from big cities when it comes to flats. De Onafhankelijken believes this does not fit in Oudewater. However, beautiful apartment buildings of a few stories do fit. There is certainly a great need for that.

121787.explanation: But with good rules and quality requirements. And maximum rental prices. These are currently lacking.

121789.explanation: In good consultation and gradually. A condition is a good alternative for parking.

121790.explanation: The cooperation is difficult despite everyone's efforts. We think we can organize the organization differently to preserve our independence in the long term. Think of a lead municipality. We invite the council to visit Renswoude, which has been doing this for years.

121791.explanation: This was pushed through administratively. We continue to talk with residents and support them in the consultations with the province.

121793.explanation: Think of temporary flexible homes, tiny houses, and water homes. We urgently need housing.

121794.explanation: The new swimming pool is necessary because the old one is worn out. If no new building comes, there will be no swimming pool left. The annual costs must remain the same for the municipality.

121743.explanation: A local (consultative) referendum is a good and applicable democratic instrument, provided the question can be answered with yes or no. Besides the referendum, there are other instruments to better involve residents in the municipality's future: participation, transparency, and a citizens' council.

121749.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing. This does not necessarily have to be on their own premises. The municipality can help if entrepreneurs seek cooperation, for example with space or permits. The costs are for the business sector.

121752.explanation: After the fireworks ban that comes into effect next year, we will search together for an attractive alternative. Progressive Oudewater would gladly allocate budget for this.

121753.explanation: Bulky waste can be offered free of charge at the recycling center in Oudewater. For residents who cannot do this themselves, bulky waste should be collected at a reasonable rate. Oudewater's minimum policy allows for customization for residents who have difficulty paying.

121754.explanation: The event policy is in order and includes noise standards. These standards must be enforced.

121756.explanation: This statement is too simplistic. Progressive Oudewater is in favor of developing farmyards into small-scale residential yards. This helps reduce the housing shortage in Oudewater and contributes to the livability of the rural area. However, this must fit the local situation.

121759.explanation: All school buildings have been recently renewed or renovated and are in good condition. It is the municipality's legal task to ensure good school buildings. So if there is reason for improvement, the municipality must allocate budget for this.

121760.explanation: Introducing children to music and culture is an essential part of every child's development and belongs in the primary school curriculum.

121763.explanation: The number of community enforcement officers has already increased in recent years. The focus should be on prevention and behavior. Think of sufficient youth work and social work. Better service from the municipality can also work preventively to prevent insecurity. Invest in better, not stricter.

121764.explanation: Repression is not the solution, and we reject a surveillance society. Prevention and behavior are more important. Nuisance from loitering youth is not prevented by cameras but by a youth center or community building.

121765.explanation: The municipality has a duty of care for the temporary shelter of residents who, due to circumstances such as domestic violence, need a safe roof over their heads. This may also be a regional solution with our neighboring municipalities.

121766.explanation: A starter loan can give our young people that extra financial room to buy a house in Oudewater. But we must also build affordable starter homes.

121769.explanation: Coercion does not help people on social assistance. Social assistance is not a choice. Experience shows that people actually want to work for their living. Therefore, the focus should be on guidance towards a job and help to overcome problems.

121771.explanation: Poverty and debts do not only occur among the lowest incomes or unemployed people. Social policy must therefore be tailored to ensure people can move forward. We support conducting a Nibud assessment of our minimum income policy. With the outcome, we can improve the policy.

121773.explanation: With the increase in the number of electric cars, more charging points are needed. Where possible, the owner should provide this themselves. The task should be addressed in conjunction with parking policy.

121774.explanation: Speed limits contribute to traffic safety and a healthy living environment. Progressive Oudewater wants a 30 km/h zone on Utrechtse- and Goudsestraatweg and the Zwier Regelinkstraat and Johan J. Vierbergenweg, with extra pedestrian crossings near Tramweg and the Lijnbaan.

121775.explanation: Professional care and support must be accessible and affordable for all residents, regardless of their background or belief.

121780.explanation: Sunday opening of shops and hospitality meets a clear need. The choice lies with the shopkeepers and entrepreneurs themselves.

121781.explanation: Status holders have a disadvantage in the housing market and therefore deserve priority in allocation. Displacement does not occur if there are sufficient social homes. Therefore, social housing must be a priority in Oudewater.

121782.explanation: All good initiatives that contribute to the necessary energy transition deserve our support.

121783.explanation: Providing school swimming places great pressure on schools, and financing swimming education for everyone is not a municipal task. If needed, the municipality can contribute to swimming lesson costs for residents who cannot afford it themselves.

121786.explanation: What is high? If well integrated, buildings with multiple floors (up to 6-7) can make more affordable homes possible on less land. It remains important to ensure a good mix in neighborhoods. High-rise buildings must not cause all cheap housing to cluster together.

121787.explanation: If the living quality is guaranteed, room rental can contribute to solving the housing shortage. Here too, room rental should not lead to a concentration of social problems in a neighborhood.

121789.explanation: Progressive Oudewater supports a center where the street belongs to pedestrians and cyclists.

121790.explanation: Oudewater must first think with residents about the municipality's future. Progressive Oudewater therefore wants to hold a citizens' council in 2027 about Oudewater's future with 100 randomly selected residents. Only then can you consider whether to collaborate with Woerden.

121791.explanation: Further resistance is pointless. The provincial project decision is final after extensive consultation with the regional municipalities. Working together with residents for good landscape integration, maximum say, and compensation offers more perspective.

121793.explanation: Building within the built-up area is preferred and we see inner-city locations where housing is possible in the longer term. In the short term, we will build homes outside the built-up area at Kerkwetering. Water homes along Goudsestraatweg (Archipel) also offer short-term opportunities.

121794.explanation: Before making a final decision about building a new swimming pool, the plan must be completed in detail. Only then can it be carefully calculated and openly assessed by the municipal council.

121743.explanation: We are not in favor of referendums.

121749.explanation: A solution must be found for the labor migrant issue. Separating work and housing is an important point. Requiring housing on one's own property does not seem to contribute to that solution.

121752.explanation: We are not in favor of municipal fireworks shows. Under the new law, associations can organize these.

121753.explanation: Collecting bulky waste is a good idea, but there must be a fee for this.

121754.explanation: We support the advancement of end times for parties in the rural area.

121756.explanation: The ChristianUnion-SGP supports farmhouses. This must be well integrated into the landscape.

121759.explanation: All school buildings are in order. Extra expenditures are only made if necessary.

121760.explanation: With the Groene Hart Pass, we help people with a low income so their children can also play sports, visit museums, or take music lessons.

121763.explanation: More community enforcement officers are needed to solve the parking problems.

121764.explanation: This is not necessary. There are enough cameras in the industrial area. In the city center, this is not needed.

121765.explanation: The municipality must stand up for the safety of women.

121766.explanation: The municipality should help starters by building cheap homes, not by making them borrow extra and get into debt.

121769.explanation: People must contribute to society if they are on social assistance.

121771.explanation: There are more and more 'working poor'. We need to develop an approach for this.

121773.explanation: Electric is the future. There must be enough charging stations.

121774.explanation: In residential streets the speed limit is 30 km/h. On distributor roads, it can be 50 km/h. Many more bike paths are needed.

121775.explanation: It is important that everyone receives the care that suits them.

121780.explanation: Sunday is the day of rest.

121781.explanation: Housing status holders can also be in flexible housing or other locations. Only if the target cannot be met is priority used.

121782.explanation: The ChristianUnion-SGP has always supported a solar park in Papekop and opposed wind turbines. That remains the case. No solar farms on other sites are allowed.

121783.explanation: For low-income people, swimming lessons should be reimbursable through the Groene Hart Pass.

121786.explanation: The ChristianUnion-SGP supports residential buildings up to 6 floors, as at Schuylenburcht.

121787.explanation: Rooming of houses often leads to nuisance, parking pressure, and other problems (for example, with labor migrants).

121789.explanation: Leeuweringerstraat must be car-free on Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Outside those days, cars can enter the streets.

121790.explanation: Oudewater must start thinking about the future in the coming years: do we continue or stop the cooperation with Woerden. The ChristenUnie-SGP also wants to explore whether we can cooperate with Lopik and Montfoort.

121791.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP has always supported a solar park in Papekop and opposed wind turbines. That will remain unchanged.

121793.explanation: We will fully get to work on Kerkwetering. This is urgently needed.

121794.explanation: After years of discussion, a very cheap swimming pool will finally be built.

statements.title: Oudewater must end its cooperation with Woerden.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Oudewater should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oudewater moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Oudewater who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Oudewater must continue to oppose wind turbines in Papekop.

titleParts.text: Oudewater moet zich blijven verzetten tegen

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Oudewater.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: High-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Oudewater.

statements.theme: Housing development outside the built-up area
statements.title: Oudewater must allow and encourage housing development outside the built-up area.

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: A women's shelter for victims of domestic violence should be established in Oudewater.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The center of Oudewater should be made car-free.
Oudewater must cut costs on plans for a new swimming pool.

titleParts.text: op de plannen voor een nieuw zwembad.
